response.justification: Bogdan has genuine distributed-systems depth from his Dunnhumby tenure (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O), consistent Staff-level IC history, strong TypeScript, and directly relevant AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq. However, Python — a stated must-have — is completely absent from his skills list and resume narrative, which is a hard gap for this role. His high-throughput systems experience is also 2018–2020 and was DLang-based rather than the Python/TypeScript stack the role centres on. If Python proficiency can be confirmed through a quick screen, he becomes a solid candidate; without it, the fit is uncertain.
strengths: Hands-on experience building microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby, directly matching the high-throughput distributed-systems requirement
Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work at re:cinq demonstrates real exposure to AI-native engineering environments — the role's baseline expectation
Repeated Staff-level IC tenure across multiple companies with evidence of ownership, TDD discipline, and agile practices (pairing, trunk-based dev, CI/CD)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills section and every job description — a stated must-have with no evidence of proficiency anywhere in the resume
The most relevant distributed-systems experience (Dunnhumby) is six years old and was implemented in DLang, raising questions about current depth in the required Python/TypeScript stack
No concrete examples of cross-functional stakeholder management, technical strategy ownership, or incident-response leadership that the Staff-level scope demands
